data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series B biotech and health company founded by Daniel Ek that has raised $326M+ to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $1.8B. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 public-sector peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in the same sector, with particular focus on 4 Series B-stage peers: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods were applicable to this analysis. The stage-progression method applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to the next funding stage, yielding $3.4B. The peer-median approach across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $805M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, a common constraint for private company valuation. The public sector shows a median P/S multiple of 7.4x but cannot be calibrated without revenue.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $684M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $2.1B across the two applicable methods. The substantial range reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer set—particularly the 5.5x spread from Profluent to Eikon—and uncertainty around stage-progression timing. The current $1.8B valuation sits near the lower-median of the derived range, suggesting either valuation stability or potential upside contingent on operational milestones.
